quick_skim.title: Implicit Neural Representations of Individual Behavior
quick_skim.abstract: We study policy representation learning from unlabeled multi-policy behavioral data. Each episode is generated by a fixed policy, but policy labels are unavailable. This setting appears in robotics play, demonstrations, games, racing, and other datasets where heterogeneous behaviors are mixed without annotations. We introduce \emph{Behavioral INR}, a self-supervised generative model that adapts implicit neural representations (INRs) from vision to behavior. Instead of mapping coordinates to RGB values, Behavioral INR represents a policy as a state-action function mapping states to subsequent actions. An episode-level latent modulates this function through FiLM layers, yielding a generative prior over policies and allowing policy identity to be inferred without supervision. Because INRs treat each datapoint as samples from an underlying function, the same model naturally accommodates variable episode lengths and different sampling granularities, as in vision INRs with different image resolutions. We also define policy-level out-of-distribution (OOD) shifts along state-distribution and action-distribution axes, which arise when policies overlap in states or actions but are not captured by standard behavioral OOD settings based only on new agents or environments. We evaluate on synthetic Gaussian random field data, MuJoCo demonstrations with controlled OOD splits, and real-world chess, Formula 1 racing, robotics, and Seek-Avoid datasets. Behavioral INR most consistently improves policy identifiability in the hardest continuous state-action settings, especially when longer episodes, more policies, and OOD splits reduce the usefulness of marginal shortcuts; amortized history encoders remain competitive when policy identity can be recovered from symbolic repetition or low-dimensional action statistics. We release code and checkpoints.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: learns policy representations from unlabeled game behavior data, applicable to studying game AI bot behaviors
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从无标签游戏行为数据中学习策略表示，可用于研究游戏AI bot行为
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从无标签游戏行为数据中学习策略表示，可用于研究游戏AI bot行为
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从无标签游戏行为数据中学习策略表示，可用于研究游戏AI bot行为
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Behavioral INR, a self-supervised method that learns implicit neural representations of policies from unlabeled behavioral data in games and robotics. By modeling policies as state-action functions and using latent variables to modulate them, it enables unsupervised policy identification and behavior generation, aiding game AI bot design.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在多策略行为数据中，每个片段由固定策略生成但缺乏策略标签，常见于游戏AI、机器人玩耍和赛车等场景。本文提出行为隐式神经表示（Behavioral INR），一种自监督生成模型，将视觉领域的隐式神经表示迁移到行为域。该方法将策略表示为状态到动作的函数，并利用集级潜变量通过FiLM层调制该函数，从而构建策略的生成式先验。这使得无需监督即可推断策略身份，并生成新的行为。实验表明，在游戏和赛车等数据集上，该方法能有效学习个体行为表征，为游戏AI bot的行为分析和拟人化设计提供了基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 在多策略行为数据中，每个片段由固定策略生成但缺乏策略标签，常见于游戏AI、机器人玩耍和赛车等场景。本文提出行为隐式神经表示（Behavioral INR），一种自监督生成模型，将视觉领域的隐式神经表示迁移到行为域。该方法将策略表示为状态到动作的函数，并利用集级潜变量通过FiLM层调制该函数，从而构建策略的生成式先验。这使得无需监督即可推断策略身份，并生成新的行为。实验表明，在游戏和赛车等数据集上，该方法能有效学习个体行为表征，为游戏AI bot的行为分析和拟人化设计提供了基础。

quick_skim.title: AgentSpec: Understanding Embodied Agent Scaffolds Through Controlled Composition
quick_skim.abstract: LLM agents are increasingly built not as single model calls, but as scaffolded systems that combine reasoning, memory, reflection, action execution, and learning. While such scaffolds often improve performance, they are often embedded in tightly coupled pipelines, making it difficult to isolate component contributions, compare alternative designs, or understand how module interactions shape agent behavior. We introduce AgentSpec, a modular specification framework that represents embodied agents as typed compositions of reusable policy components with standardized interfaces. AgentSpec standardizes the interfaces among perception, memory, reasoning, reflection, action, and optional learning, enabling components to be swapped and recombined under controlled conditions. We instantiate this framework across DeliveryBench, ALFRED, MiniGrid, and RoboTHOR, and analyze reasoning, memory, reflection, and reinforcement-learning modules across model backbones. Our results show that agent performance is governed by scaffold compatibility and interaction effects rather than isolated module strength. In particular, structured multi-granularity memory improves long-horizon state tracking, reasoning and memory interact non-uniformly across environments, reflection trades off correction and cost, and RL-trained policies compose best when optimized with deployment-time scaffold structure. AgentSpec provides a controlled foundation for studying, comparing, and designing composable LLM agents. Our code, baselines and interactive playground are publicly available at https://agentspec-embodied.github.io.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: modular agent specification for bot design, relevant to game AI
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 模块化智能体规范用于机器人设计，与游戏AI相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 模块化智能体规范用于机器人设计，与游戏AI相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 模块化智能体规范用于机器人设计，与游戏AI相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AgentSpec provides a modular framework to compose and study embodied agent scaffolds, enabling controlled experimentation in agent design.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对当前LLM智能体系统中各模块紧密耦合、难以单独评估和比较的问题，提出AgentSpec模块化规范框架，将智能体定义为感知、记忆、推理、反思、动作和学习等可替换组件的类型化组合，并标准化接口。该框架可应用于游戏AI机器人开发，通过受控组合实验帮助设计者理解模块交互对智能体行为的影响，从而构建更高效、更拟人化的游戏智能体。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对当前LLM智能体系统中各模块紧密耦合、难以单独评估和比较的问题，提出AgentSpec模块化规范框架，将智能体定义为感知、记忆、推理、反思、动作和学习等可替换组件的类型化组合，并标准化接口。该框架可应用于游戏AI机器人开发，通过受控组合实验帮助设计者理解模块交互对智能体行为的影响，从而构建更高效、更拟人化的游戏智能体。

quick_skim.title: Do programming languages still matter to your AI coding agent teammate? Evidence at scale from chess engines
quick_skim.abstract: Frontier coding agents now promise end-to-end authorship of complete software systems. Two empirical questions follow: can AI coding-agent teammates program in any target language, including ones with no comparable prior open-source artefact? If so, does language choice still shape the artefact, and along which dimensions? We study both through a polyglot case study built around chess engines: non-trivial multi-component systems that admit a hierarchy of language-agnostic oracles, from exact move-generation correctness to a strength scale (Elo), observable from Rust to Brainfuck. We prompted two frontier agents (Claude Code and Codex) at the capability level, without chess knowledge or implementation guidance, under a documented intervention and stopping policy. The agents produced 34 chess engines spanning 17 primary programming languages, from mainstream to specialised, domain-specific, legacy, and esoteric targets. We combine per-engine feature analysis, independent Elo assessment, and session trajectories with qualitative analysis of code and transcripts.   Frontier coding agents are genuinely polyglot: every language we tried produced at least one feature-rich working engine, several with no prior open-source counterpart of comparable scope (e.g., LaTeX), and the code is synthesised from scratch rather than copied. Yet language choice still matters: strong playing strength is only reachable in mainstream compiled languages, cost and engineering effort grow sharply as the language becomes more exotic, and feature choices shift across language families. Agents validate their own work unprompted, but their strength self-estimates are biased and a few engines cheated by calling a chess library. Programming language is no longer about whether AI teammates can build a working system, but about performance, cost, what gets built, and how much human supervision validation still needs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Large-scale study on AI agents building chess engines, directly relevant to game AI bot development
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 大规模研究AI智能体构建棋类引擎，直接涉及游戏AI机器人开发
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 大规模研究AI智能体构建棋类引擎，直接涉及游戏AI机器人开发

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper investigates how programming language choice affects AI coding agents' ability to develop chess engines, showing language still impacts bot performance and design, offering insights for game AI bot creation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文通过大规模棋类引擎案例，探究编程语言选择对AI编码智能体开发游戏AI机器人的影响。实验显示，即使前沿智能体也能跨语言编程，但语言仍显著影响所生成引擎的正确性与棋力，为游戏AI bot的开发提供了语言层面上的实证指导。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文通过大规模棋类引擎案例，探究编程语言选择对AI编码智能体开发游戏AI机器人的影响。实验显示，即使前沿智能体也能跨语言编程，但语言仍显著影响所生成引擎的正确性与棋力，为游戏AI bot的开发提供了语言层面上的实证指导。
